1	---
2	name: lean-vs-blueprint-checker
3	description: Per-file bidirectional verifier. Reads one .lean file and its blueprint chapter; reports (a) whether the Lean file follows the blueprint — fake/placeholder statements, signature mismatches with \lean{...} hints, proof divergence, "temporary" defs — AND (b) whether the blueprint chapter is detailed enough to have guided a faithful formalization of this file.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended in the review phase. Dispatch one checker
11	    per `.lean` file that received prover work this iteration. Each
12	    dispatch is independent and parallel-able.
13	
14	    **You may skip me this iter when NO `.lean` file received prover
15	    work** (e.g. the prover phase was skipped, or every prover
16	    INCOMPLETE'd without committing edits). Record under `## Subagent
17	    skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`:
18	    ``- lean-vs-blueprint-checker: no .lean files modified this iter
19	    (no prover edits to verify)``.
20	
21	    Do NOT skip a per-file dispatch when the prover DID commit edits
22	    to that file — skipping a prover-touched file is the review-phase
23	    failure this affordance exists to surface, not to enable. The
24	    "highly recommended" framing applies to the whole-iter decision,
25	    not to picking-and-choosing within an iter that has multiple
26	    prover-touched files.
27	  - Also dispatch on any file a code-audit subagent in your catalog
28	    flagged as suspicious, even if it received no prover work this
29	    iter.
30	  - The directive must name exactly one Lean file and its blueprint
31	    chapter — no global context, no strategy snapshot. My value is
32	    the narrow file-vs-chapter view.
33	  - I report bidirectionally: Lean → blueprint AND blueprint → Lean.
34	    The blueprint can be the failure (too thin to guide
35	    formalization, missing the level of detail the Lean code clearly
36	    needed), not only the Lean. If I flag a chapter as inadequate,
37	    the plan agent should dispatch the catalog's blueprint-writing
38	    subagent to address it.
39	  - Any must-fix-this-iter finding I report blocks downstream work on
40	    the affected file/chapter until addressed. Soft enforcement: the
41	    plan agent reads my report and follows the gate rules in plan.md.
42	---
43	
44	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
45	
46	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
47	
48	## Your Job
49	
50	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
51	
52	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
53	
54	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
55	
56	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
57	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
58	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
59	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
60	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
61	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
62	
63	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
64	
65	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
66	
67	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
68	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
69	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
70	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
71	
72	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
73	
74	## Directive Format
75	
76	```markdown
77	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker Directive
78	
79	## Slug
80	<slug>
81	
82	## Lean file
83	<path/to/file.lean>
84	
85	## Blueprint chapter
86	blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex
87	
88	## Known issues
89	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
90	```
91	
92	That's it. No strategy snapshot, no references, no full project context — just the two files and any pre-known issues to skip. Cross-file consistency is the job of the code-audit and blueprint-review subagents in the catalog; you stay focused on this one pair.
93	
94	## What you do
95	
96	1. Read your directive.
97	2. Read the Lean file completely. Note every declaration (`def`, `theorem`, `lemma`, `instance`, `axiom`, …) and its signature.
98	3. Read the blueprint chapter completely. List every `\lean{...}` reference and its corresponding declaration block.
99	4. **For each `\lean{...}` block**: locate the Lean declaration. Verify:
100	   - It exists.
101	   - Its signature matches the informal statement (use `archon-lean-lsp` for hover/signature info).
102	   - If the Lean has a body (proof or definition body), does it match the blueprint's prose? Naming and tactic choice can differ; the *mathematical content* should match.
103	5. **For each declaration in the Lean file**: is it `\lean{...}`-referenced from the blueprint? Unreferenced declarations are notable but not necessarily wrong (helpers may exist). Flag suspect ones.
104	6. **Scan for red flags** in the Lean file:
105	   - `:= sorry` where the blueprint claims a substantive statement (placeholder).
106	   - Suspect bodies like `:= True`, `:= rfl` for non-trivial claims, `:= Classical.choice _`.
107	   - Comments excusing wrong/incomplete code: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- will fix later`, `-- this is wrong but works for now`.
108	   - `axiom` declarations.
109	7. **Cross-check** any `import` paths and namespace usage that should match what the blueprint's `\lean{...}` hints suggest.
110	
111	You may use:
112	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics.
113	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to count sorries in the file.
114	
115	You may NOT modify the Lean file, the blueprint, or any other project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
116	
117	## Report format
118	
119	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
120	
121	```markdown
122	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Check Report
123	
124	## Slug
125	<slug>
126	
127	## Iteration
128	<NNN>
129	
130	## Files audited
131	- Lean: <path>
132	- Blueprint: <path>
133	
134	## Per-declaration
135	
136	For every `\lean{...}` block in the chapter, one entry:
137	
138	### `\lean{Foo.bar}` (chapter: \thm:bar)
139	- **Lean target exists**: yes / no (if no: declaration name in the .lean that comes closest, or "none found")
140	- **Signature matches**: yes / partial / no (with one line on the mismatch)
141	- **Proof follows sketch**: yes / partial / no / N/A (if N/A: chapter doesn't have a proof body to compare against)
142	- **notes**: <free-form, one line per observation>
143	
144	### `\lean{Foo.baz}` ...
145	
146	## Red flags
147	
148	(Section appears only if there are findings.)
149	
150	### Placeholder / suspect bodies
151	- `Foo.bar` at line 42: body is `:= sorry` but blueprint claims a substantive proof.
152	- `Foo.qux` at line 78: defined as `:= True`, suspect.
153	- ...
154	
155	### Excuse-comments
156	- `Foo.lean:113`: comment "TODO: replace with real def" attached to `Foo.frob`. Blueprint claims `Foo.frob` is the actual definition, not a placeholder.
157	- ...
158	
159	### Axioms / Classical.choice on non-trivial claims
160	- `Foo.lean:200`: `axiom` declaration `Foo.skip_step` introduced. Blueprint does not authorize this.
161	
162	## Unreferenced declarations (informational)
163	
164	<list of declarations in the Lean file that no `\lean{...}` references. Most are helpers — only flag the ones whose name suggests they should be in the blueprint.>
165	
166	## Blueprint adequacy for this file
167	
168	A bidirectional check: does the blueprint chapter give a prover enough detail to formalize this file correctly?
169	
170	- **Coverage**: <N>/<M> Lean declarations have a corresponding `\lean{...}` block in the chapter. Unreferenced declarations: <count> helpers (acceptable) + <count> substantive (flagged below).
171	- **Proof-sketch depth**: <one of: adequate / under-specified / silent>. If under-specified or silent, list which `\thm:...` blocks need expansion.
172	- **Hint precision**: <one of: precise / loose / wrong>. Loose hints (the prose pins one Mathlib predicate but the `\lean{...}` doesn't, leaving the prover to guess) get flagged. Wrong hints (the `\lean{...}` names a declaration whose signature doesn't match the prose) are critical.
173	- **Generality**: <one of: matches need / too narrow / too broad>. If too narrow, name the parallel API that the project ended up writing because the blueprint didn't cover the needed level of generality.
174	- **Recommended chapter-side actions**: <bullet list of items a blueprint-writing subagent should land if you flagged anything above>
175	
176	## Severity summary
177	
178	Apply these rules verbatim. Do NOT under-classify to soften the blow — the plan agent's gates depend on accurate severity, and a hidden critical finding hardens into shipped wrong code.
179	
180	- **must-fix-this-iter** — every one of the following lands here, no exceptions:
181	  - Placeholder body (`:= sorry`, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim, suspect `:= Classical.choice _` patterns) on a declaration the blueprint claims is substantive.
182	  - Signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose — including using the wrong Mathlib predicate (e.g. `Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n`) when the prose pins one specific predicate.
183	  - Excuse-comments (`-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- placeholder`, `-- wrong but works for now`) on declarations the blueprint claims are real.
184	  - Axioms or `Classical.choice _` on substantive claims the blueprint does NOT authorize.
185	  - Blueprint adequacy failure: chapter is so under-specified that a prover could not have formalized this file correctly from prose alone.
186	  - Weakened-wrong definitions: Lean defines a structurally-different stand-in (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic Γ(X,⊤)` when the blueprint defines it as an invertible quasi-coherent O_X-module).
187	- **major** — partial signature mismatches that are fixable in-place, missing `\lean{...}` references to declarations that the blueprint should reference, comments that are stale but not actively misleading.
188	- **minor** — naming drift, helpers worth promoting to blueprint blocks, low-impact prose-vs-Lean differences.
189	
190	Overall verdict: one sentence.
191	```
192	
193	The plan agent's per-file gate (see plan.md and the blueprint-review subagent's `dispatcher_notes` in the catalog) treats every must-fix-this-iter finding as blocking. If you classify a wrong signature as "major" to avoid the gate firing, you are working against the project, not for it.
194	
195	## Return value
196	
197	Your final assistant message:
198	
199	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> declarations checked, <M> red flags`
200	- The path to your full report.
201	
202	## Reminders
203	
204	- **One file pair, no global context.** Cross-file consistency belongs to the catalog's code-audit and blueprint-review subagents.
205	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
206	- **Excuse-comments are red flags, not workflow.** Treat "we use a wrong def for now" with the suspicion it deserves.
207	- **Mathematical content match, not syntactic.** The Lean proof can choose different tactics than the chapter's sketch implies, as long as the mathematical steps are the same.
208

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

13	
14	This file is the **A.1.c.SubT** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
15	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It records the dedicated
16	substrate on which the abelian-group instance of the relative Picard quotient
17	`Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) := Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` rests (the residual `sorry`
18	of `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`).
19	
20	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
21	infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
22	`[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three
23	ingredients on the Lean carrier:
24	
25	1. a binary tensor-product operation
26	   `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`;
27	2. the structure sheaf `O_X` as a designated unit object for `⊗`;
28	3. an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects, i.e.
29	   the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)` of an invertible sheaf.
30	
31	At Mathlib's pinned commit (`b80f227`), only a presheaf-level version of (1)
32	is available (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); (2) and (3) are present
33	as scheme-level objects, but the binary operation in (1) that ties them
34	together at the `Scheme.Modules` level is missing, and there is no
35	`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the
36	project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) + (3)
37	into a monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.

149	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
150	
151	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
152	
153	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
154	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
155	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
156	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
157	
158	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
159	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
160	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
161	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
162	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
163	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
164	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
165	
166	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
167	
168	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
169	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
170	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
171	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
172	
173	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
174	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
175	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
176	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
177	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
178	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
179	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
180	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
181	
182	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
183	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
184	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
185	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
186	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
187	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
188	law is definitional. -/
189	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
190	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
191	
192	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
193	
194	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
195	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the
196	underlying presheaf of `M` (the internal hom into the structure presheaf,
197	`M^∨(U) = ℋom_{𝒪_X|_U}(M|_U, 𝒪_X|_U)`).
198	
199	Construction = the **exact dual analogue of `tensorObj`** (this file, `tensorObj`):
200	apply the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 …)` on the
201	small Zariski site of `X` to the (axiom-clean, sub-step-3) presheaf dual
202	`PresheafOfModules.dual M.val`. The scheme's structure presheaf `X.presheaf` is
203	`CommRingCat`-valued over the single-universe topological site `Opens X`, hence is
204	exactly the base `R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}` that `PresheafOfModules.dual`
205	requires (the value `M^∨(U) = M|_U ⟶ R|_U` is an `R(U)`-module, needing
206	commutativity) — no CommRingCat/RingCat re-bridging is needed, since
207	`tensorObj` already takes `(R := X.presheaf)` over the same CommRingCat presheaf
208	and `X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` definitionally.
209	
210	The sheafification functor already lands in `SheafOfModules`, so no manual
211	`Presheaf.IsSheaf` / sheaf-condition descent is needed (sheafifying an already-sheaf
212	gives an iso object; this is the file's convention, matching `tensorObj`).
213	
214	Per blueprint `lem:internal_hom_isSheaf` (§`sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`); Stacks
215	tags 01CM (internal hom into a sheaf is a sheaf) / 01CR item 2. This is the
216	`⊗`-inverse candidate of an invertible sheaf, feeding `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
217	noncomputable def dual {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
218	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
219	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) :
220	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
221	
222	/-- **The sheaf-level dual is contravariantly functorial in isomorphisms.** An isomorphism
223	`e : M ≅ M'` in `X.Modules` induces `dual M' ≅ dual M`, obtained by sheafifying the presheaf-level
224	dual iso `PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso` of the underlying presheaf isomorphism. This is the
225	reusable "dual respects isos" ingredient (the dual analogue of `tensorObjIsoOfIso`) feeding the
226	assembly of `dual_isLocallyTrivial`: a trivialisation `L.restrict f ≅ 𝒪` yields, contravariantly,
227	`dual 𝒪 ≅ dual (L.restrict f)`. -/
228	noncomputable def dualIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules} (e : M ≅ M') :
229	    dual M' ≅ dual M :=
230	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
231	    (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso (R₀ := X.presheaf)
232	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e))
233	
234	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
235	
236	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
237	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
238	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
239	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
240	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
241	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
242	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
243	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
244	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
245	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
246	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
247	
248	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
249	
250	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
251	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
252	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
253	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
254	
255	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
256	
257	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
258	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
259	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
260	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
261	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
262	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
263	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
264	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
265	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
266	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
267	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
268	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
269	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
270	
271	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
272	sheaf.**
273	
274	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
275	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
276	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
277	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
278	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
279	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
280	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
281	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
282	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
283	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
284	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
285	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
286	
287	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
288	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
289	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
290	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
291	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
292	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
293	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
294	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
295	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
296	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
297	
298	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
299	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
300	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the
301	sheafification counit. -/
302	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
303	    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅ M :=
304	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
305	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
306	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
307	
308	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
309	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
310	The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `β_ M.val
311	N.val` sheafifies to the asserted isomorphism by the cheap `mapIso` pattern. -/
312	noncomputable def tensorObj_braiding {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) :
313	    tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj N M :=
314	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
315	    (BraidedCategory.braiding
316	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val)
317	
318	/-- **Associator for `⊗_X`.** (Blueprint
319	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`.)
320	For arbitrary `M, N, P : X.Modules` there is an isomorphism
321	`(M ⊗_X N) ⊗_X P ≅ M ⊗_X (N ⊗_X P)`. This is the objectwise existence-of-iso datum the
322	group law consumes (associativity as a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)`).
323	
324	**UNCONDITIONAL and axiom-clean (iter-238 ROUTE (d)).** No flatness or local-triviality
325	hypothesis is used: the earlier flatness route (`W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_flat`, needing
326	sectionwise flatness — false for a general line bundle on a non-affine open) is RETIRED.
327	Writing `a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)` and `η` the
328	sheafification-adjunction unit, the three-step composite is:
329	  1. `a(η_{M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val} ▷ P.val)` is iso, giving
330	     `(M ⊗ N) ⊗ P ≅ a((M.val⊗N.val) ⊗ P.val)`;
331	  2. `a.mapIso α : a((M.val⊗N.val)⊗P.val) ≅ a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val))`, `α` the
332	     presheaf-of-modules associator;
333	  3. `a(M.val ◁ η_{N.val ⊗ᵖ P.val})` is iso, giving
334	     `a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val)) ≅ M ⊗ (N ⊗ P)`.
335	Steps 1/3 invert the whiskered sheafification unit via the flatness-free
336	`PresheafOfModules.W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W` (η = `toSheafify ∈ J.W`, and `J.W` is
337	stable under whiskering) together with `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` (the sheafification
338	functor IS the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`). The defeq carrier bridge
339	`X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` is handled by the leading
340	`letI instMS` below. -/
341	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules} :
342	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) := by
343	  -- UNCONDITIONAL (iter-238, step 0): the locally-trivial hypotheses are dropped —
344	  -- the body never consumed them (the whiskered-unit localizer fact holds for
345	  -- arbitrary modules under ROUTE (d)). Matches the blueprint `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`
346	  -- framed unconditional and enables `tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible`.
347	  -- Bridge the monoidal structure across the `rfl`-defeq carrier
348	  -- `Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`.
349	  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
350	    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
351	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
352	  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
353	  -- Underlying presheaf tensors and the sheafification unit `η = toSheafify`.
354	  set MN := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val with hMN
355	  set NP := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) N.val P.val with hNP
356	  set η := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
357	    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with hη
358	  -- The two whiskered-unit localizer facts (ROUTE (d), via `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`).
359	  -- `η_A = toSheafify` lies in `J.W` (`W_toSheafify`).
360	  have hηMN : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
361	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN)) := by
362	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
363	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
364	  have hηNP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
365	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app NP)) := by
366	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
367	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
368	  have hW1 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
369	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN ▷ P.val)) :=
370	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := X.presheaf) P.val (η.app MN) hηMN
371	  have hW3 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
372	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (M.val ◁ η.app NP)) :=
373	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W (R := X.presheaf) M.val (η.app NP) hηNP
374	  -- Steps 1 and 3: the sheafification functor inverts the whiskered units.
375	  have hi1 : IsIso (a.map (η.app MN ▷ P.val)) :=
376	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW1
377	  have hi3 : IsIso (a.map (M.val ◁ η.app NP)) :=
378	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW3
379	  -- Step 2: the presheaf-of-modules associator, transported under `a`.
380	  have e2 := a.mapIso
381	    ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).associator M.val N.val P.val)
382	  exact (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi1).symm ≪≫ e2 ≪≫ (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi3)
383	
384	/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
385	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.
386	
387	The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
388	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
389	and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
390	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
391	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
392	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
393	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
394	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
395	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
396	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
397	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
398	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
399	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
400	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
401	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
402	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
403	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
404	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
405	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
406	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
407	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
408	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
409	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
410	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
411	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
412	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
413	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
414	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
415	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
416	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI
417	
418	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
419	
420	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
421	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
422	
423	This is the single substrate linchpin of `A.1.c.SubT` — **CLOSED, axiom-clean**
424	(iter-217). It says the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules
425	tensor) commutes with the restriction functor along an open immersion. The proof
426	is the blueprint's four-step composite:
427	  Step 1 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`): reduce `restrict` to the abstract pullback.
428	  Step 2 (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`): move the pullback inside the
429	    sheafification (sheafification commutes with pullback).
430	  Step 3: strip the outer sheafification (`.mapIso`), descending to the presheaf goal
431	    `(pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val) ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`.
432	  Step 4: close that presheaf goal by **H1 ∘ H2**:
433	    • H1 (the sole Mathlib-ABSENT ingredient, BUILT this iter): the presheaf-level iso
434	      `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, obtained from the de-sheafified presheaf
435	      `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (adjunction along the open-immersion
436	      pair `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base`) against the existing
437	      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
438	      `restrictFunctor` structure map, so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`
439	      definitionally.
440	    • H2 (strong-monoidal tensorator): `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`
441	      with `β` sectionwise the open-immersion ring ISO `f.appIso`, so `restrictScalars β`
442	      is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`, resting on the closed
443	      `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` / `restrictScalars_isIso_{μ,ε}`); the composite
444	      `μIso` is the tensorator.
445	The superseded `Localization.Monoidal` / `J.W.IsMonoidal` route is NOT used. -/
446	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
447	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
448	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
449	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
450	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
451	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
452	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
453	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
454	  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
455	  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
456	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
457	  -- `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙
458	  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
459	  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
460	  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
461	  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
462	  --    ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)`.
463	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
464	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val) ≪≫ ?_
465	  -- Step 3. **Strip the outer sheafification.** Both sides are
466	  -- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` applied to a
467	  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`, and
468	  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to
469	  -- `(M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`. So it suffices to give the
470	  -- comparison at the PRESHEAF level and sheafify it. This is a genuine reduction
471	  -- step (verified: the goal below has no sheafification).
472	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
473	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL CLOSURE — iter-217 H1 build). The remaining presheaf goal is
474	  --   `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
475	  --      ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`
476	  -- where `φ = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` and `⊗ₚ =
477	  -- PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`. We close it via:
478	  --  (H1, the linchpin) the presheaf-level iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, built from
479	  --      the presheaf `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (above) against the existing
480	  --      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
481	  --      open-immersion structure map of `restrictFunctor f`, so
482	  --      `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val` definitionally.
483	  --  (H2) the strong-monoidal comparison `(pushforward β).obj (A ⊗ₚ B) ≅
484	  --      (pushforward β).obj A ⊗ₚ (pushforward β).obj B`.
485	  -- `φR` (the scheme structure map) and `β` (the restrictFunctor structure map) are kept as
486	  -- `let`-bindings (zeta-transparent) so the unit/counit triangle goals below reduce; the
487	  -- open-immersion adjunction is INLINED for the same reason (a `have` would make `adj.unit`
488	  -- opaque and block the `congr` defeq, exactly as in Mathlib's sheaf-level `restrictAdjunction`).
489	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
490	  -- The restrictFunctor structure map `β` (so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`).
491	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
492	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
493	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
494	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
495	  -- H1 via the presheaf pushforward-pushforward adjunction + `leftAdjointUniq`.
496	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
497	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
498	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
499	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
500	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
501	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_
502	  -- H2: the strong-monoidal tensorator of `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`.
503	  -- `β` is sectionwise bijective (it is the `forget₂`-image of the open-immersion structure ring
504	  -- ISO `f.appIso`), so `restrictScalars β` is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`),
505	  -- and `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` is `Monoidal` (Mathlib); the composite's `μIso` is the tensorator.
506	  -- It is built over the SYNTACTIC `_ ⋙ forget₂` base form (where the `MonoidalCategory` instance is
507	  -- found canonically); the result is DEFEQ to the goal — whose base `X.ringCatSheaf.obj` is only
508	  -- defeq, not syntactically, `X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂` — and `(pushforward β).obj M.val =
509	  -- (M.restrict f).val` definitionally, so `exact` closes it without any instance diamond.
510	  have hβ : ∀ U, Function.Bijective (β.app U).hom := by
511	    intro U
512	    haveI : IsIso (β.app U) :=
513	      inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map (f.appIso U.unop).inv))
514	    exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (β.app U)
515	  let β' : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
516	      (f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := β
517	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β').Monoidal :=
518	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective β' hβ
519	  exact (Functor.Monoidal.μIso
520	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat f.opensFunctor X.presheaf
521	      ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β')
522	    (M.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
523	    (N.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).symm
524	
525	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
526	
527	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
528	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
529	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
530	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
531	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
532	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
533	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
534	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
535	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
536	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
537	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
538	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
539	  intro x
540	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
541	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
542	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
543	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
544	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
545	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
546	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
547	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
548	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
549	    tensorObj_unit_iso
550	
551	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the d.2-free descent re-route (B-connector)
552	
553	The "locally-iso ⇒ iso" half of the descent assembly of `exists_tensorObj_inverse`:
554	a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on an open
555	neighbourhood of every point is a global isomorphism. The route is the stalkwise
556	iso criterion `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (for sheaves valued
557	in `Ab`, whose forgetful functor reflects isos and preserves limits / filtered
558	colimits) together with `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso` (restriction along an
559	open immersion commutes with stalks). **No stalk-⊗ ("d.2") is invoked**: this is a
560	statement about a single module morphism, never about the tensor stalk. -/
561	
562	/-- **B-connector: a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on
563	an open cover is an isomorphism.** For `φ : M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`, if every point
564	`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ`
565	is an isomorphism, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This is the B-bridge of the d.2-free
566	descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
567	lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)

850	/-! ## §6. Pullback-monoidality substrate (A.1.c): `IsInvertible.pullback`
851	
852	Project-local Mathlib supplement. The relative Picard consumer re-bases onto the
853	`IsInvertible` carrier and its structure maps are *pullback* maps for GENERAL scheme
854	morphisms (the projection `C ×_S T → T` and base-change maps are neither open
855	immersions nor flat). We need that pullback preserves `⊗`-invertibility. This requires
856	`pullbackTensorIso` (`f^*(M ⊗ N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`) and `pullbackUnitIso`
857	(`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`). Blueprint §`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`. -/
858	
859	/-- **Composition coherence of the unit→pushforward-unit comparison.**
860	
861	For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X`, the canonical comparison
862	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` of the composite `h ≫ f` factors through the comparisons

1169	    inverse image is a LEFT KAN EXTENSION along `(Opens.map f.base).op` (a colimit, NOT
1170	    sectionwise), and neither `PresheafOfModules.extendScalars` nor a concrete
1171	    inverse-image-of-presheaves-of-modules functor exists in Mathlib at the pin. Building
1172	    that concrete strong-monoidal functor + its adjunction to `pushforward` is the genuine
1173	    multi-hundred-LOC obligation (Mathlib-scale; the `distribBaseChange` strong-monoidal
1174	    core exists only at `ModuleCat.extendScalars`, the topological inverse image does not).
1175	
1176	The reusable presheaf-level prerequisites toward that build are supplied just below
1177	(`PresheafOfModules.pushforward` is LAX monoidal; hence the abstract presheaf pullback is
1178	OPLAX monoidal with a canonical comparison map `δ`).
1179	
1180	**SUPERSEDED (iter-243 pivot, see §D1'–D4' below).** The general-`f` concrete-model route is
1181	ABANDONED and off-path. The relative Picard consumer only ever needs `δ` to be an iso on LINE
1182	BUNDLES, and there iso-ness comes via the **local-trivialisation chart-chase** (D2'–D4'), NOT via a
1183	concrete inverse-image model: the oplax `δ` is sheafified (`pullbackTensorMap`), reduced to the
1184	single sheafified-presheaf comparison (`isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta`), shown iso
1185	on the unit pair (D2'), and globalised over a trivialising cover (D4', `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`).
1186	The "no free oplax ⇒ preserves invertibles" obstruction (`Γ(ℙ¹,𝒪(1)) = 0`) is real for a GENERAL
1187	module but is sidestepped for line bundles by the chart-chase — no concrete model is built. -/
1188	
1189	/-- **Identifying the sheafified presheaf-pullback of `M.val` with the abstract pullback.**
1190	For `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M : X.Modules`, sheafifying the presheaf-level pullback of the
1191	underlying presheaf `M.val` recovers the abstract `𝒪`-module pullback `(pullback f).obj M`.
1192	This is the per-object form of `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback` composed with the
1193	sheafification counit on the (already-sheaf) `M`; it is the bridge used to reconcile the
1194	right-hand side of the pullback–tensor comparison `pullbackTensorMap` with the substrate
1195	`tensorObj` of the pullbacks. -/
1196	noncomputable def pullbackValIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) (M : X.Modules) :
1197	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1198	        ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom).obj M.val)
1199	      ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M :=
1200	  ((SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app M.val).symm ≪≫
1201	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).mapIso
1202	      ((asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
1203	        (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M)
1204	
1205	/-- **The sheaf-level pullback–tensor comparison map `δ_sheaf`** (blueprint
1206	`lem:pullback_tensor_map`). For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X` and arbitrary
1207	`M N : X.Modules`, the canonical comparison morphism
1208	`f^*(M ⊗_X N) ⟶ f^*M ⊗_Y f^*N`. It is the sheaf-level transport of the presheaf-level
1209	oplax comparison `δ` (`presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal`) through sheafification, assembled
1210	from the `sheafificationCompPullback` device, `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`, and `pullbackValIso`.
1211	This is a *map only*: in general it is not asserted to be an isomorphism (the invertible
1212	case is upgraded to an iso by local trivialisation in `IsInvertible.pullback`). -/
1213	noncomputable def pullbackTensorMap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) (M N : X.Modules) :
1214	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (tensorObj M N) ⟶
1215	      tensorObj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj N) := by
1216	  let φ := f.toRingCatSheafHom
1217	  let φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1218	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) := φ.hom
1219	  let a_Y := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)
1220	  refine ((SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback φ).app
1221	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)).hom ≫ ?_
1222	  refine a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val) ≫ ?_
1223	  refine (sheafifyTensorUnitIso (X := Y)
1224	      ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ').obj M.val)
1225	      ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ').obj N.val)).hom ≫ ?_
1226	  exact a_Y.map (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1227	    (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1228	    ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom)
1229	    ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f N).hom))

1231	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — D1: the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition
1232	
1233	These are general `PresheafOfModules` statements (any `F : C ⥤ D`, ring presheaves
1234	`R, S`), not specific to schemes; they are project-local because Mathlib's pinned commit
1235	exposes neither `extendScalars` nor `pullback₀` at the presheaf-of-modules level. Both are
1236	realised as the left adjoint of an existing right adjoint: `pushforward₀ F R` is
1237	definitionally `pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))` (because `restrictScalars (𝟙) = 𝟭` on the nose,
1238	witnessed by Mathlib's `restrictScalars (𝟙 R)).Full := inferInstanceAs (𝟭 _).Full`), and
1239	`restrictScalars φ` is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`; since `pushforward _` is
1240	always a right adjoint, so are these two, and their left adjoints `pullback₀`/`extendScalars`
1241	exist. The decomposition `pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀` then follows from the
1242	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ` by
1243	uniqueness of left adjoints (`Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso`).
1244	
1245	Per blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition` (D1). **OFF-PATH (iter-243 pivot).** D1 is
1246	axiom-clean and self-contained, but it was the first brick of the ABANDONED general
1247	strong-monoidal pullback build (`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`, route (e)); the active
1248	route is the loc-triv chart-chase D1'–D4' (§below), which does NOT consume `extendScalars`/
1249	`pullback₀`. Retained as a correct, reusable presheaf-level decomposition; do NOT extend it
1250	toward the general build (D2 scalar-strong / D3 topological-interchange are NOT pursued). -/
1251	
1252	section PullbackLanDecomposition
1253	
1254	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C] {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1255	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
1256	
1257	/-- `pushforward₀ F R` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))`
1258	(since `restrictScalars (𝟙) = 𝟭` on the nose). Project-local; carries the existence of the
1259	topological inverse image `pullback₀`. -/
1260	private lemma pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1261	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀.{u} F R).IsRightAdjoint :=
1262	  inferInstanceAs (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))).IsRightAdjoint
1263	
1264	/-- `restrictScalars φ` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`.
1265	Project-local; carries the existence of the extension of scalars `extendScalars`. -/
1266	private lemma restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1267	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
1268	  inferInstanceAs
1269	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (F := 𝟭 C) (R := F.op ⋙ R) φ).IsRightAdjoint
1270	
1271	/-- **The topological inverse image `pullback₀ := (pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint`**, the
1272	left adjoint of the fixed-ring pushforward. On underlying presheaves it is the left Kan
1273	extension along `F.op`. Project-local (no `PresheafOfModules` inverse-image functor at the
1274	pin); the carrier of D3. -/
1275	noncomputable def pullback0 (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1276	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} R :=
1277	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1278	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint
1279	
1280	/-- **Extension of scalars `extendScalars φ := (restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint`**, the left
1281	adjoint of restriction of scalars along a morphism of ring presheaves. Project-local; the
1282	carrier of D2 (strong monoidal via `distribBaseChange`). -/
1283	noncomputable def extendScalars (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1284	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} S ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) :=
1285	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1286	  (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint
1287	
1288	/-- The adjunction `pullback₀ ⊣ pushforward₀`. -/
1289	noncomputable def pullback0Adjunction (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1290	    pullback0 F R ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R :=
1291	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1292	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R)
1293	
1294	/-- The adjunction `extendScalars φ ⊣ restrictScalars φ`. -/
1295	noncomputable def extendScalarsAdjunction (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1296	    extendScalars φ ⊣ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ :=
1297	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1298	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ)
1299	
1300	/-- **D1 — the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition** (blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition`).
1301	For `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` presenting a pushforward of presheaves of modules, the presheaf
1302	pullback factors as extension of scalars followed by the topological inverse image,
1303	`pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀`. This is the left-adjoint reversal of the
1304	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ`, obtained
1305	from `Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso` (uniqueness of left adjoints). Project-local. -/
1306	noncomputable def pullbackLanDecomposition (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1307	    PresheafOfModules.pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback0 F R :=
1308	  (Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso
1309	    (extendScalarsAdjunction φ) (pullback0Adjunction F R)
1310	    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ)
1311	    (Iso.refl (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ))).symm
1312	
1313	end PullbackLanDecomposition
1314	
1315	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — D1'–D4' loc-triv pullback–tensor comparison
1316	
1317	The locally-trivial-restricted upgrade of the oplax comparison map
1318	`pullbackTensorMap` (`f^*(M ⊗ N) ⟶ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`) to an isomorphism, blueprint
1319	§`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`, sub-lemmas D1'–D4'. -/
1320	
1321	section LocTrivPullbackTensor
1322	
1323	/-- **The sheafified `⊗ₘ` of the two `pullbackValIso`s (piece 4 of `pullbackTensorMap`) is an
1324	iso.** Factored out as a top-level lemma so the `tensorIso (C := _ ⋙ forget₂)` elaboration mirrors
1325	the proven `tensorObjIsoOfIso` (it does not elaborate cleanly inside a tactic block carrying
1326	`extract_lets` locals). Project-local helper for `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta`. -/
1327	private lemma isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
1328	    (f : Y ⟶ X) (M N : X.Modules) :
1329	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1330	      (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1331	        (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1332	        ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom)
1333	        ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f N).hom))) :=
1334	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1335	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1336	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1337	      ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso (pullbackValIso f M))
1338	      ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso (pullbackValIso f N)))).isIso_hom

1340	/-- **Reduction of `pullbackTensorMap` iso-ness to the sheafified presheaf δ.**
1341	
1342	`pullbackTensorMap f M N` is the four-fold composite
1343	`(sheafificationCompPullback).hom ≫ a_Y.map δ ≫ (sheafifyTensorUnitIso).hom ≫ a_Y.map (tensorHom …)`.
1344	Three of the four factors (`sheafificationCompPullback`, `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`,
1345	and the `tensorHom` of the two `pullbackValIso`s) are isomorphisms unconditionally;
1346	the only conditional factor is the sheafification `a_Y.map δ` of the presheaf-level
1347	oplax comparison `δ`. Hence `pullbackTensorMap f M N` is an iso whenever that
1348	sheafified δ is an iso. This isolates the SOLE remaining content (the sheafified δ)
1349	for D2' (unit pair) and D4' (chart-chase). Project-local. -/
1350	lemma isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
1351	    (f : Y ⟶ X) (M N : X.Modules)
1352	    (h : letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1353	          (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1354	          (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1355	        IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
1356	          (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1357	          (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val))) :
1358	    IsIso (pullbackTensorMap f M N) := by
1359	  unfold pullbackTensorMap
1360	  extract_lets φ φ' a_Y
1361	  -- piece 2 (the sheafified δ) is the only conditional factor — supplied by `h`.
1362	  haveI hδ : IsIso (a_Y.map
1363	      (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val)) := h
1364	  -- pieces 1 and 3 are `Iso.hom`s; piece 4 is `a_Y.map` of the (iso) `⊗ₘ` of the two
1365	  -- `pullbackValIso`s, supplied by the factored top-level helper.
1366	  exact IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance (IsIso.comp_isIso' hδ
1367	    (IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance (isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso f M N)))
1368	
1369	/-- **Converse of `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W`.** A morphism of presheaves of modules whose
1370	image under the associated-sheaf functor is an isomorphism lies (on underlying additive presheaves)
1371	in the sheafification localizer `J.W`. This is the same morphism-property identity
1372	`PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` (the sheafification
1373	functor *is* the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`) read backwards. Project-local:
1374	needed to feed the flatness-free whiskering lemmas `W_whisker{Left,Right}_of_W` from a sheafified
1375	iso (the D2' η-bridge route, below). -/
1376	private lemma W_of_isIso_sheafification {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
1377	    {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (α : R₀ ⟶ Rsh.obj)
1378	    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
1379	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]
1380	    {A B : _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} R₀} (f : A ⟶ B)
1381	    (hf : IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).map f)) :
1382	    J.W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R₀).map f) := by
1383	  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) α
1384	  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage
1385	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α) f := hf
1386	  rw [← h] at h2
1387	  exact h2

1389	/-- **D2' δ-wrapping — the sheafified cotensorator on the unit pair is an iso, given the η-bridge.**
1390	
1391	The presheaf-level oplax unitality `Functor.OplaxMonoidal.left_unitality_hom` factors the
1392	cotensorator `δ (pullback φ') 𝟙_ 𝟙_` of the abstract presheaf pullback through the unit comparison
1393	`η (pullback φ')` (right-whiskered by `F.obj 𝟙_`) and the (iso) left unitor. Sheafifying, the
1394	unitor factor and the `F.map (λ_ 𝟙_)` factor are isomorphisms unconditionally; the whiskered
1395	`η`-factor `a_Y.map (η F ▷ F.obj 𝟙_)` is an iso whenever `a_Y.map (η F)` is — because a sheafified
1396	iso lies in `J.W` (`W_of_isIso_sheafification`), `J.W` is stable under right-whiskering
1397	(`W_whiskerRight_of_W`, flatness-free), and `J.W`-morphisms sheafify to isos
1398	(`isIso_sheafification_map_of_W`). Hence the sheafified `δ` on the unit pair is an iso, which is
1399	exactly the hypothesis the reduction brick `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` consumes
1400	on `M = N = 𝒪`. Project-local; the **δ-wrapping** half of D2' (`lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit`),
1401	self-contained modulo the η-bridge `IsIso (a_Y.map (η (pullback φ')))`. -/
1402	lemma isIso_sheafifyDelta_unitPair_of_isIso_sheafifyEta {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
1403	    (h : letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1404	          (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1405	          (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1406	        IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1407	          (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')))) :
1408	    letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1409	        (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1410	        (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1411	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1412	      (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')
1413	        (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).val (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).val)) := by
1414	  letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1415	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1416	      (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1417	  set a_Y := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
1418	  change IsIso (a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') (𝟙_ _) (𝟙_ _)))
1419	  set F := PresheafOfModules.pullback φ' with hF
1420	  have hWη : (Opens.grothendieckTopology (Y : TopCat)).W
1421	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η F)) :=
1422	    W_of_isIso_sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val) _ h
1423	  have hWw : (Opens.grothendieckTopology (Y : TopCat)).W
1424	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η F ▷ F.obj (𝟙_ _))) :=
1425	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := Y.presheaf) _ _ hWη
1426	  haveI hIsoW : IsIso (a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η F ▷ F.obj (𝟙_ _))) :=
1427	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hWw
1428	  haveI hIsoLam : IsIso (a_Y.map (λ_ (F.obj (𝟙_ _))).hom) := inferInstance
1429	  have hBC : IsIso (a_Y.map
1430	      (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η F ▷ F.obj (𝟙_ _) ≫ (λ_ (F.obj (𝟙_ _))).hom)) := by
1431	    rw [Functor.map_comp]; infer_instance
1432	  haveI hD : IsIso (a_Y.map (F.map (λ_ (𝟙_ _)).hom)) := inferInstance
1433	  have hlu := Functor.OplaxMonoidal.left_unitality_hom F (𝟙_ _)
1434	  have key : a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ F (𝟙_ _) (𝟙_ _)) ≫
1435	      a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η F ▷ F.obj (𝟙_ _) ≫ (λ_ (F.obj (𝟙_ _))).hom)
1436	      = a_Y.map (F.map (λ_ (𝟙_ _)).hom) := by
1437	    rw [← Functor.map_comp]; exact congrArg _ hlu
1438	  have heq : a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ F (𝟙_ _) (𝟙_ _))
1439	      = a_Y.map (F.map (λ_ (𝟙_ _)).hom) ≫
1440	        inv (a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η F ▷ F.obj (𝟙_ _) ≫ (λ_ (F.obj (𝟙_ _))).hom)) := by
1441	    rw [← key, Category.assoc, IsIso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
1442	  rw [heq]; infer_instance
1443	
1444	/-- **D2' assembly — `pullbackTensorMap` on the unit pair is an iso, given the η-bridge.**
1445	Chains the δ-wrapping `isIso_sheafifyDelta_unitPair_of_isIso_sheafifyEta` into the reduction brick
1446	`isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` (on `M = N = 𝒪`). This is the full statement of
