3840	  \uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal, lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}
3841	  Equality of presheaf morphisms is checked sectionwise. Over each open both presheaf-level
3842	  maps are the single ring map \(\varphi.\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,X\): the presheaf lax unit
3843	  of a pushforward (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}) is the structure-map
3844	  comparison \(\mathcal{O} \to \varphi_*\mathcal{O}\), and the underlying presheaf component of
3845	  \(\mathtt{unitToPushforwardObjUnit}\,\varphi\) is the same comparison by construction --- its
3846	  value on a section is \(\varphi.\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,X\) (the same comparison whose
3847	  composition coherence is \cref{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}). Since the two presheaf
3848	  morphisms agree on every section, they are equal. Tracing the sheafification reconciliation
3849	  backwards then identifies this presheaf-level identity with the step~6 presheaf head
3850	  \(\varepsilon(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi')\) of \cref{lem:eta_bridge_unit_square}, closing
3851	  $(\ast\ast)$.
3852	\end{proof}

3856	% of \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv} is the specialisation \(h := j'\).
3857	% \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) is not an adjunction transpose (it is a four-fold

3868	  Let \(h : Z \to Y\) and \(f : Y \to X\) be composable morphisms of schemes and let
3869	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). Write \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}} =
3870	  \mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) for the sheaf-level pullback--tensor comparison of
3871	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}, \(h^{*}(-) = (\mathtt{pullback}\,h).\mathrm{map}\) for
3872	  the pullback functor, and \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f\) for the pullback
3873	  pseudofunctoriality isomorphism \((h \circ f)^{*} \cong h^{*}\circ f^{*}\). Then the
3874	  comparison for the composite \(h \circ f\) factors through the comparisons for \(f\)
3875	  and for \(h\), conjugated by \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f\):
3876	  \[
3877	    \delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}^{\,h \circ f}(M, N)
3878	      \;=\;
3879	      (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{inv}_{M \otimes_X N} \;\mathbin{;}\;
3880	      h^{*}\!\bigl(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}^{\,f}(M, N)\bigr) \;\mathbin{;}\;
3881	      \delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}^{\,h}(f^{*}M,\, f^{*}N) \;\mathbin{;}\;
3882	      \mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\bigl((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f)_M,\,
3883	        (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f)_N\bigr).\mathrm{hom},
3884	  \]
3885	  an equality of morphisms \((h \circ f)^{*}(M \otimes_X N) \to (h^{*}f^{*}M)
3886	  \otimes_Z (h^{*}f^{*}N)\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,Z\), where
3887	  \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\) is the functoriality of \(\otimes\) in a pair of
3888	  isomorphisms.
3889	
3890	  \emph{Remark (the open-immersion base-change square).} The base-change form consumed
3891	  downstream by \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv} is the \emph{specialisation} of
3892	  this coherence. Given \(f : Y \to X\), an open \(U \subseteq X\) with preimage
3893	  \(f^{-1}U\), the restriction \(g : f^{-1}U \to U\) and the open immersions
3894	  \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\), \(j' : f^{-1}U \hookrightarrow Y\), the morphism
3895	  \(f^{-1}U \to X\) admits the two equal factorisations \(j' \circ f = g \circ j\)
3896	  (i.e. \(j' \mathbin{;} f = g \mathbin{;} j\)). Applying the displayed coherence to
3897	  each factorisation and equating the two results identifies the restriction
3898	  \((j')^{*}\bigl(\delta^{f}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\bigr)\) with
3899	  \(\delta^{g}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j^{*}M, j^{*}N)\), through the pseudofunctoriality
3900	  isomorphisms; in particular each is an isomorphism if and only if the other is. This
3901	  is the form used in the chart-chase, where \(M, N\) are trivialised on \(U\).
3902	\end{lemma}

4012	  opening, the \(\delta = \mathtt{homEquiv}^{-1}\bigl((\eta\otimes\eta)\mathbin{;}\mu\bigr)\)
4013	  pivot, the \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_pullbackComp\_inv}\) transport across the adjunctions
4014	  (with \(\mathtt{unit\_conjugateEquiv}\) and \(\mathtt{Adjunction.comp\_unit\_app}\)), and
4015	  the two-argument \(\mathtt{tensorHom}\)/\(\delta_{\mathrm{natural}}\) bookkeeping --- which
4016	  follows the template of Mathlib's
4017	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Adjunction.isMonoidal\_comp}\) (the canonical ``a composite of
4018	  monoidal adjunctions is monoidal'') --- are all carried out, and together they discharge
4019	  every part of Sq2b \emph{except} the single residual
4020	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp\_lax\_}\mu\). This reduction is the content of
4021	  \cref{lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}'s \(\delta\)-mirror.
4022	
4023	  Second --- and contrary to what the unit case might suggest --- this residual is
4024	  \emph{not} definitional. The unit twin \(\mathtt{unitToPushforwardObjUnit\_comp}\) holds
4025	  by reflexivity only because the unit comparison \(\eta\) touches the counit
4026	  \(\varepsilon\) alone; the \(\mu\)-version is the \emph{full} tensorator interchange and
4027	  does not collapse to a reflexivity. Read sectionwise it exposes the base-change
4028	  associativity coherence for the composite ring map versus the two-step composite, and its
4029	  proof is a genuine \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) change-of-rings calculation assembled from the
4030	  Mathlib primitives \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp}\),
4031	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.extendScalarsComp}\), and
4032	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.homEquiv\_extendScalarsComp}\). It is, in other words, the genuine
4033	  ``\(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) is monoidal'' theorem --- the right-adjoint counterpart of
4034	  the \(\delta\)-coherence already in hand --- and it is the sole remaining content of Sq2b
4035	  beyond the (completed) mate-calculus reduction.
4036	
4037	  Crucially, this sub-step is stated and proved at the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\)
4038	  level --- about \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi'\) with \(\varphi'\) the ring map
4039	  \((\mathcal{S}_{0}\mathbin{;}\mathtt{forget}_{2}) \to f^{\mathrm{op}}\mathbin{;}
4040	  (\mathcal{R}_{0}\mathbin{;}\mathtt{forget}_{2})\) carried by
4041	  \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) --- and \emph{not} at the scheme/\(\mathtt{forget}_{2}\)
4042	  level. Working at the presheaf level offers three advantages over a scheme-level
4043	  statement: the \(\mathtt{forget}_{2}\) monoidal instances are fixed by \(\varphi'\);
4044	  the factorisation through a composite functor comes for free from the
4045	  \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) signature; and the Sq2 ring-map reconciliation is
4046	  definitional.
4047	
4048	  The observation above --- that \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) is not an adjunction
4049	  transpose --- concerns the \emph{full} four-fold composite and does not constrain
4050	  Sq2b: the inner tensorator \(\delta\) genuinely \emph{is} a transpose
4051	  (\(\mathtt{homEquiv}^{-1}\) of \((\eta\otimes\eta)\mathbin{;}\mu\)), so for Sq2b
4052	  specifically the \(\eta\to\delta\) mirror of \cref{lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp} is the
4053	  correct route.
4054	
4055	  \emph{Sq1 (sheafification \(\leftrightarrow\) pullback).} The composition coherence of
4056	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\) across \(h \circ f\) --- the
4057	  analog of \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) for the ``sheafify-then-pullback'' natural
4058	  isomorphism --- is \emph{absent from Mathlib} and is supplied as a standalone project
4059	  sub-lemma (mate-calculus style).
4060	
4061	  \emph{Sq3 (unit-iso transport).} The monoidal-unit sheafification isomorphism
4062	  \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) is carried through the same \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)
4063	  identification used for Sq2.
4064	
4065	  \emph{Sq4 (the connecting iso).} The scheme-level composition coherence of
4066	  \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) relates \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,(h \circ f)\,M\) to
4067	  \(h^{*}\bigl(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,M\bigr)\),
4068	  \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,h\,(f^{*}M)\), and the pseudofunctoriality component
4069	  \((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f)_M\); it produces the final
4070	  \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\bigl((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f)_M,
4071	  (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f)_N\bigr)\) factor. This coherence is likewise \emph{absent
4072	  from Mathlib} and is the second standalone project sub-lemma.
4073	
4074	  The genuinely missing ingredients are therefore the Mathlib-absent monoidality Sq2b
4075	  (\(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)) together with the composition coherences Sq1
4076	  (\(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\)) and Sq4 (\(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\)); the Sq2
4077	  ring-map reconciliation is definitional (\(\mathtt{rfl}\)) and carries no proof
4078	  obligation. The adjunction-mate bridge
4079	  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_pullbackComp\_inv}\) (relating \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) for
4080	  the left adjoints to \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) for the right adjoints) is the device
4081	  used inside Sq2b and Sq4 to transport the pseudofunctoriality coherence across the
4082	  adjunctions. With Sq1, Sq2b, and Sq4 in hand, the Sq2 oplax decomposition and the Sq3
4083	  unit-iso transport assemble the four squares into the displayed identity. The base-change-square
4084	  consequence (the Remark) is then the specialisation \(h := j'\) obtained by equating the
4085	  two factorisations \(j' \mathbin{;} f = g \mathbin{;} j\); the iff-clause is immediate,
4086	  an isomorphism being preserved and reflected by the pseudofunctoriality isomorphism it
4087	  is conjugated by.
4088	\end{proof}

4091	[Pullback commutes with $\otimes$ on locally-trivial pairs (D4$'$)]
4092	  \label{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv}
4093	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial}
4094	  \uses{def:IsLocallyTrivial, lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural,
4095	        lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit, lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange,
4096	        lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
4097	        lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback}
4098	  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and let
4099	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be \emph{locally trivial}
4100	  (\cref{def:IsLocallyTrivial}). Then the sheaf-level comparison map
4101	  \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}} = \mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) of
4102	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map} is an isomorphism:
4103	  \[
4104	    f^*(M \otimes_X N)
4105	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
4106	    (f^*M) \otimes_Y (f^*N),
4107	  \]
4108	  natural in \(M\) and \(N\). This is the comparison the relative Picard consumer needs;
4109	  it is established by promoting the already-built map \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) to an
4110	  isomorphism through a chart-chase, with no comparison for arbitrary modules and no
4111	  filtered-colimit machinery.
4112	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
4113	  % \ref{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} (pullback commutes with the tensor product,
4114	  % functorially) and Lemma \ref{lemma-pullback-locally-free} (the pullback of a locally
4115	  % free module is locally free --- the geometric content licensing the chart-chase)
4116	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L2392-2400 and L2112-2118).

4131	  % be a morphism of ringed spaces. If $\mathcal{G}$ is
4132	  % a locally free $\mathcal{O}_Y$-module, then

4135	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemmas
4136	  \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} and \texttt{lemma-pullback-locally-free}:
4137	  pullback commutes with \(\otimes\) functorially, and the pullback of a locally free
4138	  (locally trivial) module is again locally free; on locally-trivial pairs the comparison
4139	  is therefore an isomorphism, checkable on a trivialising cover.}

4141	
4142	\begin{proof}
4143	  \uses{def:IsLocallyTrivial, lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural,
4144	        lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit, lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange,
4145	        lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
4146	        lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback}
4147	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Lemmas \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} and
4148	  \texttt{lemma-pullback-locally-free}.}
4149	
4150	  By local triviality of \(M\) and \(N\) (\cref{def:IsLocallyTrivial}) choose a common
4151	  affine open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) on which both restrict to the trivial bundle,
4152	  \(M|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) and \(N|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) --- the
4153	  common refinement of the two trivialising covers, exactly as in the proof that the
4154	  tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial
4155	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}). The preimages
4156	  \(\{f^{-1}U_i\}\) form an open cover of \(Y\). It suffices, by the
4157	  restriction-detects-isomorphism criterion \cref{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}, to show
4158	  that the canonical map \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\) restricts to an isomorphism on
4159	  each \(f^{-1}U_i\); the canonical global map being shown locally-iso is exactly what
4160	  lets us conclude (a non-canonical patchwork of local isomorphisms would not glue).
4161	
4162	  Fix \(i\) and write \(g_i : f^{-1}U_i \to U_i\) for the restriction of \(f\), with open
4163	  immersions \(j_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\) and \(j_i' : f^{-1}U_i \hookrightarrow Y\),
4164	  so \(f \circ j_i' = j_i \circ g_i\). The base-change coherence
4165	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange} identifies the restriction
4166	  \((j_i')^*\delta^{f}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\) with the comparison
4167	  \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j_i^*M, j_i^*N)\) for \(g_i\) on the restricted modules,
4168	  and reduces the claim to: \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j_i^*M, j_i^*N)\) is an
4169	  isomorphism. On \(U_i\) the restricted modules are trivial, \(j_i^*M \cong
4170	  \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) and \(j_i^*N \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\); by naturality of
4171	  \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) in both arguments (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural})
4172	  these isomorphisms transport \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j_i^*M, j_i^*N)\) to the
4173	  comparison on the unit pair \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(\mathcal{O}_{U_i},
4174	  \mathcal{O}_{U_i})\). That comparison is an isomorphism by
4175	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit} --- the unit-pair case, where the structure-sheaf
4176	  comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\) (an isomorphism for all \(g_i\)) and the unitors
4177	  do the work. Hence \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j_i^*M, j_i^*N)\) is an isomorphism,
4178	  so is its base-change identification \((j_i')^*\delta^{f}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\), and
4179	  thus \(\delta^{f}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\) restricts to an isomorphism on each
4180	  \(f^{-1}U_i\). (Both sides are indeed locally trivial on this cover --- \(f^*M, f^*N,
4181	  f^*(M\otimes_X N)\) are locally trivial by \cref{lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback} and
4182	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial} --- which is what makes the chart-local
4183	  comparison a map between copies of the structure sheaf.) By
4184	  \cref{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}, \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\) is a global
4185	  isomorphism, natural in \(M, N\) by \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural}.
4186	\end{proof}

## Severity summary

### Major findings

**Major #1 — stale Sq2b proof description (blueprint → Lean)**

Blueprint prose (lines ~4027–4035) describes `pushforwardComp_lax_μ` as proved by "a genuine `ModuleCat` change-of-rings calculation assembled from the Mathlib primitives `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp`, `ModuleCat.extendScalarsComp`, and `ModuleCat.homEquiv_extendScalarsComp`."

The **committed proof** (Lean lines 2246–2291) uses:
- `pushforward_μ_eq` — a `rfl` lemma; reduces `μ (pushforward φ)` to `μ (restrictScalars φ')`
- `restrictScalars_μ_app` — a `rfl` lemma (sectionwise form)
- `forget₂_restrictScalars_μ_hom_tmul` — pure-tensor collapse via `ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul` (inner leg)
- `pushforward_map_restrictScalars_μ_app_tmul` — pure-tensor collapse via `ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul` (outer leg, reindexed by `pushforward_map_app_apply`)

None of `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp`, `ModuleCat.extendScalarsComp`, or `ModuleCat.homEquiv_extendScalarsComp` appear in the proof. The proof does not use extension of scalars at all; it is a pure sectionwise + pure-tensor collapse on the `restrictScalars` μ. The blueprint both overstates the difficulty (by implying an extendScalarsComp construction is needed) and names primitives the proof does not use.

The declaration is already closed so this is not blocking, but it is stale and misleading to any reader trying to understand the proof structure.

**Major #2 — stale "Sq2b still missing" status (blueprint → Lean)**

Blueprint lines ~4074–4085 list "monoidality Sq2b" as one of three remaining genuinely missing ingredients for `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`. As of iter-259/261, Sq2b is **axiom-clean**: `pullbackComp_δ` (mate-calculus, ~90 lines) and its sole residual `pushforwardComp_lax_μ` are both proved with no sorry and no axioms. Only Sq1 (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback` composition coherence) and Sq4 (`pullbackValIso` composition coherence) remain open.

A prover dispatched to work on `pullbackTensorMap_restrict` reading this passage would wrongly believe Sq2b needs work. The passage should be updated to reflect that only Sq1 and Sq4 are open.

### Overall verdict

Two major blueprint-prose inaccuracies: (1) wrong Mathlib primitives named for the committed `pushforwardComp_lax_μ` proof, (2) Sq2b listed as still missing when it is axiom-clean. Neither blocks downstream provers immediately (the closed declarations are done; the open obligations are `exists_tensorObj_inverse` and `pullbackTensorMap_restrict` Sq1/Sq4 which are correctly documented). No must-fix-this-iter issues found; 2 declarations with sorry bodies (`exists_tensorObj_inverse`, `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`) are known open obligations, not placeholder violations.
